# 3.4 Communication Delay and Computation Delay

Let sr represent the total occupied storage capacity of RSU r. Then, sr can be calculated as,

sr = sdr + sir.

An inference request can be processed by different RSUs with varying accuracy level models. For example, a request with a medium accuracy requirement can be processed by the corresponding medium or high accuracy models. Thus we define a binary indicator θmp to represent whether the task ajk is processed by model πmp. Specifically, θmp = 1 indicates that the task is processed by the model and vice versa.

The RSU is able to communicate with each other through a wired connection, so that inference tasks can be offloaded from one RSU to others. This means that the RSU to which an inference task is initially submitted may not be the same as the RSU where the task is ultimately offloaded for execution. Thus, transmission delay is introduced only when the task is offloaded to a different RSU. Let Ttrans denote the transmission delay for offloading the inference task ajk from original RSU ojk to RSU r. When task ajk is scheduled to RSU r (i.e., yjkr = 1), the Ttrans can be expressed as follows:

Ttrans =

djk/Brsu,     if ojk = r,

0,         otherwise,

where Brsu is the transmission rate between RSUs.

Let Tproc denote the computation delay for processing inference task ajk. When task ajk is scheduled to RSU r (i.e., yjkr = 1), Tproc is defined by,

Tproc =

(θmp · wgpu)/fgpu,     if zjk = 1,

(θjk · wcpu)/fcpu,     otherwise,

We define a binary indicator hmpr to represent whether RSU r has already cached the model. Specifically, hmpr = 1 denotes that RSU r has cached the mth model with accuracy level p and vice versa. Let Tdown denote the communication delay for RSU r to download the mth model with accuracy level p from the cloud. Thus, Tdown is defined by,

Tdown =

cmp/Bcloud,     if xmpr = 1 and hmpr = 0,

0,         otherwise,

where Bcloud is the transmission rate between the cloud and RSU.

We use empr to indicate whether the mth model with accuracy level p is loaded on the GPU of RSU r. Here, if RSU r has loaded the model from CPU to GPU, empr = 1, otherwise, empr = 0. When using a GPU for inference, there is
